query: What is Elon Musk's current net worth according to Bloomberg Billionaires Index in early July 2026? Has it recovered above $1 trillion after the late June 2026 tech selloff? Include recent Tesla and SpaceX share price and valuation trajectory.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of what I found:

**Elon Musk's net worth in early July 2026: ~$1.1 trillion, still marginally above $1 trillion.**

Key findings:
- **Bloomberg Index**: $1.01T (June 2026); **Forbes**: $1.1T; an SEC-filing-based tracker showed **~$1.10T as of July 3, 2026**.
- He became the **world's first trillionaire on June 12, 2026** (SpaceX IPO day, ticker SPCX), peaked at ~$1.4T on June 16, and has dipped under $1T several times since.
- **The late-June tech selloff** cut into his fortune (SPCX fell from $211 to $153; Tesla to a $375 low on June 25). Forbes noted Musk was one of only *two* top-10 billionaires to gain over the prior month, buoyed by the SpaceX IPO windfall rather than Tesla.
- **Recovery status**: He remains above $1 trillion, but the cushion is thin. A **–7.5% Tesla drop on July 2** (to $393.45) was roughly offset by a **+2.8% SPCX rebound** (to $162.00), keeping him near $1.1T.
- His wealth is now driven **more by SpaceX (~42% stake, ~$800B)** than Tesla.

**Important caveat**: The exact Bloomberg close-of-day July figure is paywalled and couldn't be retrieved directly; figures are triangulated from Wikipedia, Forbes, trackers, and live market data (which I pulled directly for TSLA and SPCX).

sources.title: Bloomberg Billionaires Index - Elon Musk

key_facts: Elon Musk net worth early July 2026: ~$1.1 trillion; SEC-filing tracker showed ~$1.10T as of July 3, 2026 — remains marginally above $1 trillion.
Bloomberg Billionaires Index listed him at $1.01 trillion (June 2026); Forbes at $1.1 trillion.
Musk became the world's first trillionaire on June 12, 2026, the day SpaceX (ticker SPCX) began trading on Nasdaq.
His net worth peaked as high as $1.4 trillion (~June 16, 2026) and has dropped under $1 trillion several times since the IPO.
Forbes (July 1, 2026) noted Musk was one of only two of the top-10 richest to gain wealth over the prior month amid a broad tech-stock selloff.
Tesla (TSLA) closed July 2, 2026 at $393.45, down –7.49% in one day from $425.30 on July 1; market cap ~$1.48 trillion.
SpaceX (SPCX) closed July 2, 2026 at $162.00, up +2.83%; market cap ~$1.75–2.1 trillion; IPO priced ~$135/share raising ~$75B.
During the late-June selloff, SPCX fell from a $211.39 peak (June 16) to $153.00 (June 25); Tesla hit a recent low of $375.12 (June 25).
Musk owns ~42% of SpaceX (largest holding, ~$800B) and ~13.8% of Tesla plus 304M 2018 vested options (strike $23.33, restored by Delaware Supreme Court Dec 19, 2025).
Tesla 52-week range $288.77–$498.83 (currently ~21% below high); SPCX 52-week range $147.11–$225.64 (currently ~28% below high).

data_points.metric: Elon Musk net worth (Bloomberg Billionaires Index)
